revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Taga-taisha]][[el:Τάγκα-τάισα]][[en:Taga-taisha]][[fr:Taga-taisha]][[ja:多賀大社]][[tr:Taga-taisha]][[zh:多賀大社]] [[co:commons:Category:Taga-taisha]][[el:Τάγκα-τάισα]][[en:Taga-taisha]][[fr:Taga-taisha]][[ja:多賀大社]][[tr:Taga-taisha]][[zh:多賀大社]] [[el:Τάγκα-τάισα]][[en:Taga-taisha]][[fr:Taga-taisha]][[ja:多賀大社]][[tr:Taga-taisha]][[zh:多賀大社]] {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Taga-taisha
多賀大社 | image = Taga taisha1.JPG | caption = ''[[Honden]]'' of Taga-taisha | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|13|27|N|136|17|26|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = [[Izanagi]], {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} | established = Pre-{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | location = 604 Taga, Inukami-gun, Shiga-ken 522-0341 | website = {{URL|http://www.tagataisya.or.jp/}} | footnotes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Palace of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} [[File:Taga-taisha Haiden-b.jpg|thumb|280px|Haiden]] {{nihongo|'''Taga-taisha'''|多賀大社}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the town of {{ill|Taga, Shiga|en|Taga, Shiga|WD=Q1346747|lt=Taga|1=Taga, Shiga}}, {{ill|Inukami District, Shiga|en|Inukami District, Shiga|WD=Q1131103|lt=Inukami District|1=Inukami District, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is frequently referred to as {{nihongo|O-Taga-san|お多賀さん}} by local residents.{{cite web |title=Taga Taisha official home page |url=http://www.tagataisya.or.jp/about/ |website=About Us |publisher=Taga Taisha}}{{in lang|ja}} The gardens of the inner ''{{ill|Shoin|en|Shoin|WD=Q3482442|lt=Shoin|1=Shoin}}'', which date to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} are a {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=nationally designated|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} [[Monuments of Japan|Place of Scenic Beauty]],{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1575 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120803171529/https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1575 |url-status=dead |archive-date=3 August 2012 |title=多賀神社奥書院庭園 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=10 February 2012 }}{{Cite web|title=THE 10 BEST Things to Do Near Taga Taisha, Taga-cho - Tripadvisor|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com/AttractionsNear-g1121270-d1992498-Taga_Taisha-Taga_cho_Inukami_gun_Shiga_Prefecture_Kinki.html|access-date=2021-06-05|website=www.tripadvisor.com|language=en}} whereas the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} ''Shoin'' itself is a Shiga Prefectural Important Cultural Property. ==Main ''kami''== * [[Izanagi]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} ==History== The foundation of Taga Taisha is unknown. It is mentioned in the ancient {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}} and {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}} chronicles from 712 AD. The shrine's legend connects it with the legendary hero {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, whose son, {{ill|Inugami no Mitasuki|en|Inugami no Mitasuki|WD=Q11571158|lt=Inugami no Mitasuki|1=Inugami no Mitasuki}} was sent as an emissary to {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Sui China|1=Japanese missions to Sui China}} in 614 AD and the first {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Japanese embassy to Tang China|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} in 630 AD. He was also the ancestor of the Inukami clan, from which {{ill|Inukami District, Shiga|en|Inukami District, Shiga|WD=Q1131103|lt=Inukami District, Shiga|1=Inukami District, Shiga}} (where the shrine is located) takes its name. However, in the 914 AD ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, it is listed only as a small shrine. From the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} it was revered as having efficiency for childbirth, vocational aid, fire extinguishing and longevity. During then {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, as pilgrimages to the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and the [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Sanzan]] gained in popularity, pilgrims were encouraged to visit Taga Shrine as part of the route. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} had a strong faith in this shrine and frequently prayed for an extension of his mother's life. He donated 10,000 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'', or the equivalent revenue of one year for a ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' for its reconstruction and upkeep. The shrine was destroyed by a fire in 1615, but was rebuilt immediately by {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|en|Tokugawa Hidetada|WD=Q314464|lt=Tokugawa Hidetada|1=Tokugawa Hidetada}} and reconstructed in 1633 by {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}. In 1651, {{ill|Ii Naotaka|en|Ii Naotaka|WD=Q877175|lt=Ii Naotaka|1=Ii Naotaka}} of {{ill|Hikone Domain|en|Hikone Domain|WD=Q1062225|lt=Hikone Domain|1=Hikone Domain}} granted it estates of 150 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. However, much of the shrine was destroyed again by fire in 1733, and by a storm in 1791. Each time, it was rebuilt with the assistance of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] and Hikone Domain. With the establishment of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] in 1871. It was promoted to a {{nihongo3|Imperial shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei Chūsha]]}} in 1885 and to a {{nihongo3|Imperial shrine, 1st Rank|官幣大社|Kanpei-taisha}} in 1914. Many of the shrine's buildings are Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Taga Town.{{cite web |title=Registered Cultural Properties |url=https://www.town.taga.lg.jp/akebono/bunkazai/shitei.html |website=Taga Town Cultural Properties Center |publisher=Taga Town}}{{in lang|ja}} ==Festivals== *{{nihongo|'''Korei-taisai'''|古例大祭}}: Most important festival held on April 22. Also known as {{nihongo|'''Taga-matsuri'''|多賀まつり}}. *{{nihongo|'''Otaue-sai'''|御田植祭}}: A festival of planting rice with traditional rites held on June 7. *{{nihongo|'''Mantō-sai'''|万灯祭}}: A lantern festival held on the night of August 3–5. ==Specialty goods== *{{nihongo|'''Otaga-shakushi'''|お多賀杓子}}: A talisman ''shakushi'' (Japanese {{ill|Ladle (spoon)|en|Ladle (spoon)|WD=Q193358|lt=ladle|1=Ladle (spoon)}}) from the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}. Allegedly the origin of the Japanese word ''{{ill|Tadpole|en|Tadpole|WD=Q168525|lt=otamajakushi|1=Tadpole}}''. *{{nihongo|'''Itokiri-mochi'''|糸切餅}}: A ''{{ill|Wagashi|en|Wagashi|WD=Q1063096|lt=Japanese sweet|1=Wagashi}}'' made of ''{{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|WD=Q470519|lt=Mochi|1=Mochi}}'' sticky rice sold at souvenir shops around the shrine. ''Itokiri-mochi'' was first made to celebrate the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Japanese victory over the Mongols|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.{{Citation needed|date=November 2009}} ==Access== The shrine is about a 20 minute walk from {{ill|Taga Taisha-mae Station|en|Taga Taisha-mae Station|WD=Q965382|lt=Taga Taisha-mae Station|1=Taga Taisha-mae Station}} on the {{ill|Ohmi Railway|en|Ohmi Railway|WD=Q3276327|lt=Ohmi Railway|1=Ohmi Railway}} {{ill|Ohmi Railway Taga Line|en|Ohmi Railway Taga Line|WD=Q7081105|lt=Taga Line|1=Ohmi Railway Taga Line}}, or about ten minutes by bus from {{ill|Minami-Hikone Station|en|Minami-Hikone Station|WD=Q1042827|lt=Minami-Hikone Station|1=Minami-Hikone Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}. ==See also== *{{ill|Takamiya-juku|en|Takamiya-juku|WD=Q3514166|lt=Takamiya-juku|1=Takamiya-juku}}, the closest of the {{ill|69 Stations of the Nakasendō|en|69 Stations of the Nakasendō|WD=Q741218|lt=69 Stations of the Nakasendō|1=69 Stations of the Nakasendō}} to the shrine ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Taga-taisha}} * [http://www.tagataisya.or.jp/ Official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{Izanagi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Taga-taisha]] {{Commons category|Taga-taisha}} [[el:Τάγκα-τάισα]] [[fr:Taga-taisha]] [[ja:多賀大社]] [[tr:Taga-taisha]] [[zh:多賀大社]] {{wikidata link|Q402091}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Nagare-zukuri]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
